prompt:
The AST output-equivalence relation in @prune/equivalence judges semantically DIFFERENT programs "equivalent". Reproducible examples:
- `const x = 1` vs `let x = 1` vs `var x = 1` are all judged equivalent (scoping differs).
- `/foo/g` vs `/bar/i` are judged equivalent (regex text/flags ignored).
- `\`hello \${x}\`` vs `\`goodbye \${x}\`` are judged equivalent (template static text ignored).
- `-x`, `!x`, `+x`, `~x` are judged equivalent, as are `x++` vs `x--` (unary operators conflated).
- Alpha-renaming is applied to FREE identifiers too, so `if(c){a()}else{b()}` is judged equivalent to the version with the branch calls swapped.

This relation gates cost transforms: a false "equivalent" can mask a real quality regression, while a false "different" merely forgoes a saving. Fix the blindness cases, and make the identifier-exact (rename-intolerant) comparison the DEFAULT; keep alpha-rename tolerance available only as an explicit opt-in for scoring, never as a ship gate.
notes: Reference: tcrp harden AST equivalence (5 semantic-blindness bugs) 2026-06-01.
